message:
chore(beads): mark bd-40lhe render gauntlet as in_progress

Update issue status to reflect render gauntlet framework completion
progressing with the harness modules now implemented.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2023,7 +2023,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3vm0b","title":"Edge-case tests for theme.rs (ThemeId, ColorToken, contrast, accent_gradient, SemanticSwatch)","description":"Background
- CI/gates run `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`.
- `crates/ftui-extras/src/theme.rs` contains edge-case unit tests that intentionally exercise trait coverage (Debug/Hash/Copy/Clone), but several tests currently call `.clone()` on types that are `Copy`, triggering `clippy::clone_on_copy` under `-D warnings`.

Why This Matters
- This blocks the primary quality gate for the whole workspace.
- We want to keep the *coverage intent* of the tests without disabling clippy or weakening lint standards.

Scope
- Update only the affected tests in `crates/ftui-extras/src/theme.rs` so they remain meaningful while being clippy-clean.
- Do NOT relax lint settings. Do NOT delete tests.

Implementation Notes
- Replace `let c = a.clone();` with `let c = a;` (Copy assignment) and keep the semantic assertions.
- Add a short comment where helpful: `// Copy implies Clone; clippy forbids clone_on_copy`.
- If explicit Clone-trait coverage is still desired, prefer a compile-time bound assertion over runtime `.clone()` calls (but note: `Copy: Clone`, so this is typically redundant).

Reference Failure
- Current clippy errors (example):
  - `ThemeId` clone_on_copy at theme.rs:1716
  - `ColorToken` clone_on_copy at theme.rs:1757
  - `AlphaColor` clone_on_copy at theme.rs:1798
  - `StatusBadge` clone_on_copy at theme.rs:2074
  - `PriorityBadge` clone_on_copy at theme.rs:2085
","acceptance_criteria":"1) `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` passes (no `clone_on_copy` from `crates/ftui-extras/src/theme.rs`).
2) `cargo test` passes for the workspace.
3) Test intent is preserved: we still cover Debug/Hash/Copy semantics for the relevant theme types without suppressing lints.","notes":"Fresh-eyes check: this bead was previously closed as "done", but the workspace clippy gate currently fails on clone_on_copy in these tests. Reopened to track the real blocker.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"OliveGrove","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:51:28.084752892Z","created_by":"GrayCat","updated_at":"2026-02-10T04:16:10.726398968Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T04:16:10.726379371Z","close_reason":"Clippy gate: remove clone_on_copy from theme.rs edge-case tests; cargo clippy -D warnings + cargo test pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["edge-case","ftui-extras","test"],"comments":[{"id":716,"issue_id":"bd-3vm0b","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: Clippy gate failing: ftui-extras/src/theme.rs tests call .clone() on Copy types (ThemeId, ColorToken, AlphaColor, StatusBadge, PriorityBadge). Need to adjust tests to be clippy-clean under -D warnings.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:06:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vm0b.1","title":"Fix clone_on_copy in theme.rs edge-case tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletTower","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:09:28.552169761Z","created_by":"ScarletTower","updated_at":"2026-02-10T04:11:07.779087990Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T04:11:07.779067932Z","close_reason":"Workspace clippy is clean now (no clone_on_copy in theme.rs); no further changes needed for this slice","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vm0b.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3vm0b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3w4s","title":"Implement ResizeDebouncer with configurable delay","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:51.706577264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:38:10.761729618Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:38:10.761710051Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: ResizeDebouncer struct with configurable delay exists in crates/ftui-runtime/src/program.rs (handle_resize_at, tick_at, time_until_apply methods). Used in ProgramConfig with configurable resize_debounce Duration.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3w4s","depends_on_id":"bd-28jm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-40lhe","title":"task(render-perf): build render equivalence, replay, and tail-latency gauntlets","description":"Background:\nRender performance changes will only be safe if they are continuously checked against visible equivalence and tail-latency regressions. A dedicated gauntlet is the place where certificate work, presenter minimization, and layout reuse are forced to prove themselves under the canonical fixtures.\n\nGoal:\nCreate the render-specific validation suite that turns optimization claims into durable evidence.","design":"Work packages:
- Define the render gauntlet scenarios, including sparse updates, large invalidations, resize storms, style-heavy screens, and adversarial challenge cases that stress the limits of certificate and cache assumptions.
- Require focused unit/property tests for certificate logic and invalidation rules before full-system gauntlets run.
- Require replay and snapshot evidence plus scripted E2E scenarios that prove visible equivalence under each scenario.
- Capture tail-latency metrics and identify which optimization levers improve or degrade p95/p99.
- Ensure failure artifacts make it easy to see whether the problem was stale certificates, presenter divergence, cache invalidation, challenge-fixture fragility, or missing log coverage.
- Make the gauntlet reusable for future render changes, not just the first wave.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Render changes cannot be considered complete without unit/property coverage, visible-equivalence checks, and scripted E2E/replay tail-latency gates.
2. Failure artifacts are diagnostic, not merely binary, and include detailed mismatch logs and replay pointers.
3. The gauntlet becomes the standing safety net for future render optimization work.
4. The gauntlet explicitly includes challenge fixtures and negative-control cases so a win on curated render workloads is not enough by itself.","notes":"This bead is where the render lane proves it is an engineering program rather than a benchmark-chasing exercise.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:30.038901575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T19:24:38.609053486Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","ftui-render","performance","render","tail-latency","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-40lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-760ih","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:36.475427933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-40lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-ddruy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:27.524492989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-40lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-dwrhv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:30.534120615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-40lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-i71od","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:24.528429014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-40lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-js3c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:33.463714879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-40lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-muv6p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:23:20.468486839Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-40lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-pou7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:06:00.468970334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

A dedicated gauntlet is the place where certificate work, presenter minimization, and layout reuse are forced to prove themselves under the canonical fixtures.\n\nGoal:\nCreate the render-specific validation suite that turns optimization claims into durable evidence.","design":"Work packages:

- Require focused unit/property tests for certificate logic and invalidation rules before full-system gauntlets run.

The gauntlet explicitly includes challenge fixtures and negative-control cases so a win on curated render workloads is not enough by itself.","notes":"This bead is where the render lane proves it is an engineering program rather than a benchmark-chasing exercise.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:30.038901575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:46:33.594897362Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","ftui-render","performance","render","tail-latency","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-40lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-760ih","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:36.475427933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-40lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-ddruy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:27.524492989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-40lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-dwrhv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:30.534120615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-40lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-i71od","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:24.528429014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-40lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-js3c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:33.463714879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-40lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-muv6p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:23:20.468486839Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-40lhe","depends_on_id":"bd-pou7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:06:00.468970334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-4cwfj","title":"Debug Overlay: Layout/Routing Diagnostics","description":"Background
- Complex layout decisions need explainable debugging tools.

Scope
- Build overlay panel showing layout phases, crossings, routing cost, tier, guard triggers.
- Add hotkeys to toggle diagnostics and highlight nodes/edges/routes.
- Display config snapshot + capability profile summary.
- Hook into evidence logs for JSONL export.

Considerations
- Overlay must not affect layout determinism.
- Keep UI readable at small sizes (compact mode).

Tests / Validation
- Snapshot tests for overlay on/off at multiple sizes.
- PTY E2E toggling overlay with JSONL evidence to ensure stable metrics.

Success Criteria
- Overlay renders without crashing or corrupting UI.
- Diagnostics align with logged evidence and guard triggers.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:41:10.912407509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T14:44:11.837653915Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T14:44:11.837633046Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4cwfj","depends_on_id":"bd-12d5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4cwfj","depends_on_id":"bd-2fp5r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4cwfj","depends_on_id":"bd-2hxl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4cwfj","depends_on_id":"bd-315zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":717,"issue_id":"bd-4cwfj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claiming. LilacLynx here — I built the LayoutTrace/LayoutStageSnapshot types and evidence logging (bd-12d5s) that this bead depends on. Starting implementation of the debug overlay panel.","created_at":"2026-02-05T14:34:20Z"},{"id":718,"issue_id":"bd-4cwfj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LilacLynx: Implemented debug overlay (bd-4cwfj). Changes:

**mermaid.rs:**
- Added `debug_overlay: bool` to `MermaidConfig` (default: false)
- Fixed formatting issue in pie_title_text mapping

**mermaid_render.rs (~200 lines):**
- `DebugOverlayInfo` struct: diagnostic data (fidelity, crossings, bends, ranks, score, symmetry, compactness, nodes, edges, clusters, budget_exceeded, ir_hash_hex)
- `collect_overlay_info()`: gathers metrics from layout + evaluate_layout()
- `render_debug_overlay()`: renders stats panel in top-right corner with layout quality metrics
- `build_overlay_lines()`: builds label-value pairs including budget warning when exceeded
- `render_overlay_bbox()`: faint bounding box outline around diagram content
- `render_overlay_ranks()`: faint rank boundary lines between Sugiyama ranks
- `emit_overlay_jsonl()`: evidence event with all overlay metrics
- Integration in `render_diagram_adaptive()`: renders overlay + emits JSONL when `config.debug_overlay=true`
- 8 new tests: overlay_info_collects_metrics, overlay_lines_include_core_metrics, overlay_lines_show_budget_warning, overlay_renders_without_crash, overlay_skipped_when_area_too_small, overlay_adaptive_renders_with_debug_enabled, overlay_bbox_renders_at_reasonable_size, overlay_ranks_renders_at_reasonable_size

Quality gates: cargo fmt ✓, cargo check ✓, clippy -D warnings ✓, 394 tests passed (0 failed)","created_at":"2026-02-05T14:44:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4f8e1","title":"Add unit tests for layout_debugger.rs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:38:01.587323936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T03:38:11.410322248Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T03:38:11.410302591Z","close_reason":"Added 22 new unit tests (25 total). Covers: LayoutConstraints (new, unconstrained, overflow/underflow), LayoutRecord (fields, with_child, overflow/underflow), LayoutDebugger (enable/disable/clear/record), export_dot (empty, quotes, nested), render_debug (disabled, overflow colors, underflow colors).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-4flji","title":"task(runtime-perf): instrument executor, subscription, queue, and shutdown dynamics","description":"Background:\nRuntime optimization decisions should be driven by a detailed picture of queue growth, executor latency, subscription churn, process waits, and shutdown behavior. Without that, it is too easy to ship a scheduler tweak that looks clever but mainly moves pain around.\n\nGoal:\nCreate the measurement layer that exposes the runtime's real load dynamics and lifecycle costs.","design":"Work packages:
- Define the runtime metric vocabulary: queue depth, enqueue/dequeue timing, task/service latency, cancellation timing, shutdown drain behavior, and subscription/process transition reasons.
- Reuse the shared observability schema and replay-friendly logging contracts instead of inventing a runtime-only telemetry dialect.
- Tie runtime metrics back to fixture IDs and baseline IDs.
- Call out which runtime metrics are leading indicators of user pain versus implementation detail noise.
- Make it explicit how later control-policy beads should interpret these signals.
- Ensure the instrumentation plan remains compatible with deterministic replay and does not become an opaque logging firehose.
- Require the instrumentation surface to support both focused test assertions and scripted stress/E2E evidence generation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Future runtime work can reason about load and lifecycle behavior using a stable measurement contract.
2. The bead distinguishes operationally meaningful metrics from low-value telemetry.
3. The task text is sufficient to guide both implementation and review of runtime instrumentation work.
4. The instrumentation work is incomplete without unit/integration checks for metric semantics, scripted stress/E2E collection paths, and detailed machine-readable logs that later policy, comparator, and rollout beads can consume directly.","notes":"This bead is foundational because queueing/control ideas are only useful if the observed state is trustworthy and interpretable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:30.629251163Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:33:27.024888733Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T00:33:27.024353552Z","close_reason":"Runtime dynamics instrumentation in effect_system.rs + subscription.rs: 7 new counters (sub_starts/stops/panics, reconcile_count/duration, shutdown_duration/timeouts), RuntimeDynamics snapshot struct, recording hooks wired into reconcile()/stop_all()/catch_unwind. Reconcile tracks elapsed time; stop_all records shutdown duration + timeout count. 6 new dynamics tests + all 10 lifecycle tests green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","ftui-runtime","observability","performance","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4flji","depends_on_id":"bd-17ar5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:17:48.254307525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4flji","depends_on_id":"bd-760ih","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:42.499493635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4flji","depends_on_id":"bd-9v2se","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:39.480099537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4flji","depends_on_id":"bd-p8i4s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:59:45.497790811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -2155,12 +2155,12 @@
 {"id":"bd-czn4y","title":"E2E: Text effects determinism sweep","description":"Scope: E2E sweep over text effects (gradient, wave, glitch, typewriter, matrix/ocean presets) to verify deterministic rendering. Capture JSONL logs with effect name, frame index, hash, dims, mode, seed, and elapsed.\n\nConsiderations: Deterministic seeds and fixed time steps; ensure animation frame selection is repeatable; avoid flakiness.\n\nAcceptance: Script runs in CI (80x24, 120x40), logs per-effect hashes, and results are stable across runs.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Sweep runs for each text effect in alt + inline at 80x24 and 120x40.
2) JSONL logs include effect, frame index, hash, dims, mode, seed, and elapsed.
3) Hashes stable across runs with fixed seeds/time steps.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:27:40.778285598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T02:32:46.231660539Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T02:32:46.231641964Z","close_reason":"Implemented text effects sweep script + run_all integration","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-czn4y","depends_on_id":"bd-22n80","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-czn4y","depends_on_id":"bd-2txo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-czn4y","depends_on_id":"bd-3isi7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-cznw0","title":"feature(validation): deterministic harnesses, shadow-run evidence, and benchmark gates","description":"Background:
The proposed integration will only be worth shipping if we can prove it behaves the same or better under deterministic, replayable tests. That requires more than ordinary unit tests: it requires lab-style timing control, shadow comparison, benchmark gates, and artifact contracts.

Goal:
Build the validation substrate that makes the migration trustworthy and repeatable.","design":"Feature scope:
- Lab-style deterministic harnesses for runtime shell work.
- Shadow-run comparison for old vs new execution lanes.
- Traceability from invariants to specific tests and artifacts.
- User-visible benchmark gates.
- Replay/triage-friendly CI artifact contracts.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every major invariant can be traced to explicit unit, integration, E2E, and replay evidence.
2. Benchmark results map to user/operator outcomes, not vanity metrics.
3. Failure artifacts are rich enough for one-command triage and replay.","notes":"Without this feature, the rest of the plan would be implementation churn with weak proof.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-10T01:14:03.957375231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T15:48:54.839148561Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T15:48:54.838725491Z","close_reason":"Implemented shadow_run.rs (ShadowRun/ShadowRunConfig/ShadowVerdict) and benchmark_gate.rs (BenchmarkGate/Threshold/Measurement/GateResult) in ftui-harness. 19 tests pass, clippy clean. Wired into lib.rs with re-exports.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync-frankentui","benchmark","replay","test","validation"]}
 {"id":"bd-d3sfy","title":"task(runtime-perf): build runtime shadow comparison and saturation comparator","description":"Background:\nBecause runtime changes can affect ordering, timing, and shutdown behavior in subtle ways, side-by-side comparison is one of the safest ways to catch regressions. We want a comparator that can replay identical workloads through old and new runtime policies and produce structured mismatch evidence.\n\nGoal:\nCreate the runtime shadow/saturation comparison suite that verifies behavior before broader adoption.","design":"Work packages:
- Define the workload scenarios that should be compared side by side, including steady-state, bursty, cancellation-heavy, shutdown-heavy, challenge-path, and negative-control cases.
- Emit structured comparison artifacts covering event sequences, timing summaries, queue dynamics, degraded-mode transitions, recovery milestones, and mismatch reason codes.
- Make clear which differences are acceptable and which are blockers using the user-visible degraded-mode and recovery contract rather than purely internal scheduler intuition.
- Include saturation scenarios that stress the runtime near or beyond its calibrated load envelope.
- Ensure replay instructions are part of the artifact contract for failed comparisons.
- Require the comparator to explain whether a mismatch is semantic, policy-level, observability-related, or a bounded graceful-fallback difference that remains within the declared user contract.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Runtime policy changes can be evaluated against the previous behavior under controlled conditions.
2. Comparison failures are rich enough to explain the root-cause class quickly.
3. The comparator remains reusable as the runtime evolves.
4. The bead is incomplete without targeted comparator tests, scripted saturation/shadow E2E runs, and detailed mismatch logs with replay commands and reason codes.
5. Comparator verdicts must be traceable back to the user-visible degraded-mode and recovery contract so acceptable differences are explicit rather than subjective.","notes":"Planning correction: this comparator is not just a validation idea; it needs to be an operator-usable script and artifact producer so regressions can be reproduced outside local reasoning. It should also speak the same user-visible contract language as the runtime UX bead so verdicts do not drift.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:32.503077697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T20:48:07.277191832Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","ftui-runtime","performance","runtime","shadow-run","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-d3sfy","depends_on_id":"bd-4flji","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:00.574647841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-d3sfy","depends_on_id":"bd-js3c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T19:00:03.553475059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-ddruy","title":"task(render-perf): minimize presenter bytes and terminal state churn with explicit equivalence rules","description":"Background:\nEven when diff work is already minimal, the presentation layer can burn time and syscalls by emitting redundant cursor movement, style transitions, or fragmented writes. These costs matter especially for tail latency and remote or slower terminals.\n\nGoal:\nOptimize the presenter path by reducing emitted bytes and state churn while preserving terminal-visible equivalence.","design":"Work packages:\n- Identify the dominant sources of redundant presenter output under the canonical fixtures.\n- Specify what counts as terminal-visible equivalence versus harmless ANSI variation.\n- Introduce one primary compression/minimization lever, such as stronger segment batching or redundant state suppression, with explicit proof obligations.\n- Define transcript-style replay or comparison artifacts for debugging terminal divergence.\n- Call out terminal-mode interactions that require conservative handling.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Presenter optimization remains grounded in explicit equivalence rules rather than optimistic byte shaving.
2. The task text is specific enough that future implementation can be reviewed against a crisp scope boundary.
3. The bead highlights why this work matters to both average throughput and p95/p99 behavior.
4.  {"id":"bd-dez5","title":"Implement Render Budget unit and property tests","description":"# Implement Render Budget unit and property tests

## Background
Part of Render Budget Enforcement (bd-1iqn). Comprehensive tests ensure budget enforcement works correctly.

## Unit Tests
- remaining_budget_decreases_over_time: Verify remaining() returns less as time passes
- degradation_level_ordering_is_correct: Verify Full < SimpleBorders < NoStyling < EssentialOnly < Skeleton < SkipFrame
- degrade_advances_to_next_level: Verify degrade() moves to next level
- degrade_stops_at_skip_frame: Verify degrade() at SkipFrame stays at SkipFrame
- upgrade_returns_to_previous_level: Verify upgrade() moves to previous level
- should_degrade_returns_true_when_cost_exceeds_remaining: Test budget decision logic
- exhausted_returns_true_when_skip_frame_or_zero_budget: Test exhausted() edge cases

## Property Tests
- degrade_upgrade_roundtrip: Degrading then upgrading returns to same or lower level
- budget_remaining_never_exceeds_total: remaining() is always <= total
- degradation_is_monotonic: Multiple degrade() calls never decrease level

## Integration Tests
- widget_respects_degradation_level: Panel/Text widgets adjust output based on level
- system_recovers_after_budget_exhaustion: After overload, system eventually upgrades back

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Property tests find no counterexamples
- [ ] Integration tests verify widget behavior
- [ ] Test coverage >= 90% for RenderBudget module","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:32:00.093642605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:17:55.980270238Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:17:55.980251403Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-1iqn in budget.rs - 34 tests covering degradation ordering, upgrade/downgrade roundtrip, remaining never exceeds total, exhausted edge cases","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dez5","depends_on_id":"bd-1iqn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-dyr5a","title":"task(perf-foundation): define opportunity scoring, one-lever discipline, and optimization admission rules","description":"Background:\nEven with baselines and profiles in place, the team still needs a decision rule for what is worth implementing. The opportunity-matrix discipline from extreme optimization is useful here because it discourages low-EV tuning and forces implementers to justify impact, confidence, and effort before changing code.\n\nGoal:\nCreate the optimization policy bead that decides when a candidate is allowed to proceed and what evidence must accompany it.","design":"Work packages:\n- Define a scoring rubric using impact, confidence, and effort, plus any supporting relevance/risk dimensions required for alien-artifact work.\n- Specify the minimum score or policy gate needed for a candidate to move forward.\n- Require one primary lever per implementation bead unless a documented exception is approved.\n- Record what rollback, proof, and re-profile artifacts must be attached before and after implementation.\n- Explain how lower-scoring but strategically necessary work can be handled without collapsing the discipline for everything else.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Future contributors have a self-contained decision policy for whether an optimization is worth implementing.\n2. The bead makes the tradeoffs around high-impact/high-risk work explicit instead of informal.\n3.  {"id":"bd-e211","title":"Implement Text wrapping and truncation with ZWJ/emoji/combining mark correctness","description":"# Implement Text Wrapping and Truncation with Width Correctness

## Background
Plan Section F and Chapter 11 emphasize that text wrapping and truncation must be WIDTH-CORRECT for ZWJ sequences, emoji, and combining marks. This is a non-negotiable for Unicode correctness.

## Problem Statement
Breaking text at arbitrary byte or codepoint boundaries corrupts display:
- ZWJ sequences (👨‍👩‍👧) are single graphemes but multiple codepoints
- Combining marks (é = e + ́) must stay with base char
- Regional indicators (🇺🇸) are pairs of codepoints
- CJK characters are 2 cells wide

## Requirements

### Wrapping Rules
1. Never break inside a grapheme cluster
2. Respect cell width (CJK = 2 cells)
3. Handle ZWJ sequences as atomic units
4. Word-wrap at whitespace when possible
5. Character-wrap as fallback for long words

### Truncation Rules
1. Truncate at grapheme boundary only
2. Ellipsis (…) counts as 1 cell
3. If content + ellipsis > width, drop graphemes until it fits
4. Never leave partial grapheme displayed

## Implementation

### Text Wrapping
```rust
pub fn wrap_text(text: &str, width: usize) -> Vec<String> {
    let mut lines = Vec::new();
    let mut current_line = String::new();
    let mut current_width = 0;
    
    for grapheme in text.graphemes(true) {
        let grapheme_width = unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(grapheme);
        
        if current_width + grapheme_width > width {
            // Line full, start new line
            if !current_line.is_empty() {
                lines.push(std::mem::take(&mut current_line));
            }
            current_width = 0;
        }
        
        current_line.push_str(grapheme);
        current_width += grapheme_width;
    }
    
    if !current_line.is_empty() {
        lines.push(current_line);
    }
    
    lines
}
```

### Text Truncation
```rust
pub fn truncate_text(text: &str, max_width: usize, ellipsis: &str) -> String {
    let text_width = unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(text);
    
    if text_width <= max_width {
        return text.to_string();
    }
    
    let ellipsis_width = unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(ellipsis);
    let target_width = max_width.saturating_sub(ellipsis_width);
    
    let mut result = String::new();
    let mut current_width = 0;
    
    for grapheme in text.graphemes(true) {
        let grapheme_width = unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(grapheme);
        
        if current_width + grapheme_width > target_width {
            break;
        }
        
        result.push_str(grapheme);
        current_width += grapheme_width;
    }
    
    result.push_str(ellipsis);
    result
}
```

## Test Cases Required

### Wrapping Tests
- [ ] ASCII text wraps at word boundaries
- [ ] CJK text wraps correctly (each char = 2 cells)
- [ ] Emoji never split mid-sequence
- [ ] ZWJ family emoji (👨‍👩‍👧‍👦) wrap as unit
- [ ] Combining marks stay with base (é)
- [ ] Regional indicators (🇺🇸) wrap as pair
- [ ] Long words wrap mid-word correctly
- [ ] Empty lines preserved

### Truncation Tests
- [ ] Short text unchanged
- [ ] Long ASCII truncated with ellipsis
- [ ] CJK truncated at grapheme boundary
- [ ] Emoji truncated correctly
- [ ] ZWJ sequence truncated as unit
- [ ] Ellipsis fits within width
- [ ] Edge case: width < ellipsis width

### Comprehensive Corpus (from bd-16k)
- [ ] All ASCII printable characters
- [ ] Common CJK (日本語, 中文, 한국어)
- [ ] Common emoji (🎉, ❤️, 🔥, 😀)
- [ ] ZWJ sequences (👨‍💻, 👩‍🔬, 🏳️‍🌈)
- [ ] Skin tone modifiers (👋🏻, 👋🏿)
- [ ] Combining diacritics (é, ñ, ü)
- [ ] Variation selectors
- [ ] Zero-width characters (ZWSP, ZWNJ)

## Dependencies
- Uses unicode-segmentation crate for graphemes()
- Uses unicode-width crate for width measurement
- Integrates with LRU width cache (bd-1oz)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] wrap_text() is grapheme-correct
- [ ] truncate_text() is grapheme-correct
- [ ] All test cases pass
- [ ] Integrated into Text type (bd-2uk)
- [ ] Performance: < 10µs for typical text (cached)

## Location
`ftui-text/src/wrap.rs`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:20:21.625908161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:00:12.477632832Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:00:12.477600401Z","close_reason":"Implemented text wrapping module with WrapMode (None/Word/Char/WordChar), truncation with grapheme correctness, and Unicode width helpers. 32 new tests.  {"id":"bd-ic6i","title":"Internationalization (i18n) Foundation","description":"## Overview
Provide infrastructure for multi-language and RTL (right-to-left) text support in FrankenTUI applications.

## Rationale
- Global user base needs localized interfaces
- RTL languages (Arabic, Hebrew) require proper text rendering and layout mirroring
- String externalization enables translation workflows
- Locale-aware formatting (dates, numbers) improves UX

## Core Components
1. **String Catalog System**: Externalized string storage with key-based lookup
2. **RTL Layout Mirroring**: Automatic flex/grid direction reversal for RTL locales
3. **Unicode BiDi Algorithm**: Proper bidirectional text rendering in cells
4. **Locale Context**: Thread-local or model-level locale state
5. **Pluralization Rules**: ICU-style plural forms for different languages

## Implementation Strategy
- Create ftui-i18n crate (optional feature-gated dependency)
- Integrate with ftui-text for BiDi text segment handling
- Modify ftui-layout to respect text direction context
- Provide macro for compile-time string extraction

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for BiDi text rendering (Arabic, Hebrew mixed with English)
- Layout tests for RTL flex/grid mirroring
- Snapshot tests comparing LTR vs RTL layouts
- Integration tests with demo showcase in multiple locales

## Success Criteria
- Applications can switch locale at runtime without restart
- RTL layouts render correctly with proper alignment
- No performance regression for LTR-only applications

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-ic6i.1","title":"String Catalog System","description":"Externalized string storage with key-based lookup.

## Requirements
- String catalog file format (TOML/JSON)
- Key-based lookup with fallback chain
- Plural forms support (ICU style)
- Interpolation for dynamic values

## Proposed API

```rust
pub struct StringCatalog {
    locales: HashMap<Locale, LocaleStrings>,
    fallback_chain: Vec<Locale>,
}

pub struct LocaleStrings {
    strings: HashMap<String, StringEntry>,
}

pub enum StringEntry {
    Simple(String),
    Plural(PluralForms),
}

pub struct PluralForms {
    pub zero: Option<String>,
    pub one: String,
    pub two: Option<String>,
    pub few: Option<String>,
    pub many: Option<String>,
    pub other: String,
}

impl StringCatalog {
    pub fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Self, I18nError>;
    pub fn get(&self, locale: &Locale, key: &str) -> Option<&str>;
    pub fn get_plural(&self, locale: &Locale, key: &str, count: i64) -> Option<&str>;
    pub fn format(&self, locale: &Locale, key: &str, args: &[(&str, &str)]) -> Option<String>;
}

// Macro for ergonomic usage
macro_rules! t {
    ($key:expr) => { ... };
    ($key:expr, $count:expr) => { ... };
    ($key:expr, $($arg:ident = $val:expr),*) => { ... };
}
```

## File Format (TOML)

```toml
[meta]
locale = "en-US"
fallback = "en"

[strings]
greeting = "Hello"
items_count = { one = "{count} item", other = "{count} items" }
welcome = "Welcome, {name}!"
```

## File: crates/ftui-i18n/src/catalog.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_simple_lookup: Basic key retrieval
- test_fallback_chain: es-MX -> es -> en
- test_plural_english: 0, 1, 2, many
- test_plural_russian: Complex Slavic rules
- test_interpolation: Variable substitution
- test_missing_key: Graceful handling

## Plural Rule Implementation
Use CLDR plural rules. Implement for:
- English (one/other)
- Russian (one/few/many/other)
- Arabic (zero/one/two/few/many/other)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: String Catalog System
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-ic6i.2","title":"Locale Context Provider","description":"Runtime locale state management.

- set_locale(), current_locale()
- Auto-detect from LANG/LC_ALL

- Widget-level override
- System detection
